title: Lathyrus scopolii
taxonomy: Lathyrus scopolii is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is a perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region. The species is also known as the Balkan meadow-pea. Lathyrus scopolii is classified in the genus Lathyrus and is closely related to other species in the same genus. The exact classification may vary depending on the taxonomic system used.
uses: Lathyrus scopolii, also known as Scopolia's peavine, is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. However, specific information on its medicinal uses is limited. The plant contains various compounds with biological activities, which may contribute to its medicinal potential. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's benefits and applications. More studies on Lathyrus scopolii's effects on human health are required to establish its medicinal uses.
properties: Lathyrus scopolii has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Its extracts have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anticancer properties, making it a subject of interest in traditional medicine. The plant's compounds have been found to possess neuroprotective effects, which may help in the management of neurodegenerative diseases. However, more research is needed to fully understand its therapeutic potential. Further studies are required to isolate and characterize the bioactive compounds responsible for its medicinal properties.
constituents: Lathyrus scopolii contains a range of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids. These compounds are responsible for the plant's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids present in Lathyrus scopolii have been shown to exhibit potential neuroprotective and cardiovascular benefits. Additionally, the plant's phenolic acids have been reported to possess antimicrobial and antifungal activities. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential applications of Lathyrus scopolii.
preparations: Lathyrus scopolii preparations are not commonly discussed, however, Lathyrus scopolii is sometimes used in traditional herbal medicine, but its use is not well-documented. The plant's properties and potential uses are not well-researched, and its preparations are not widely available or recommended by medical professionals. More research is needed to understand the potential benefits and risks of Lathyrus scopolii preparations. The safety and efficacy of Lathyrus scopolii herbal preparations have not been extensively studied or confirmed.
side_effects: Lathyrus scopolii is known for its potential side effects, which include neurolathyrism, a condition affecting the nervous system. Consuming large amounts of the plant's seeds can lead to this condition, characterized by muscle weakness, tremors, and other neurological problems. The risk of neurolathyrism is higher when the seeds are consumed as a primary source of protein over a prolonged period. The condition is often irreversible and can cause long-term disability. It is essential to note that the risk of side effects can be minimized by consuming the plant in moderation and as part of a balanced diet.
intro: Lathyrus scopolii, also known as the Autumn-larkspur, is a perennial flowering plant belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis and menstrual cramps. The bioactive constituents of Lathyrus scopolii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lathyrus scopolii include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to calm nervous system and relieve muscle spasms.
